Definition: species as named in Zilles & Rehkamper 1988 (the orang-utan; the only species in this table)
Note: single-specimen table: brain structures down the rows, Pongo sp. the only species; taxonomy applied later via ../_keys/Stephan/

Definition: brain component as printed in Table 12-2 (indentation in the snapshot marks the printed sub-components)
Note: squished to a clean label by the R script

Definition: fresh volume of the structure as printed (cc3 = cm3)
Note: original printed unit; converted to mm3 (x1000) in volume_mm3

Definition: fresh volume converted to the project unit mm3 (= fresh_volume_cc3 x 1000)
Note: one value per structure; the per-structure rows below carry the Measure tag

Definition: structure volume as a percentage of total brain volume (excluding ventricles and nerves)
Note: printed in Table 12-2 col 2; relative measure (recomputable from volumes); not the focus of pooling

Definition: medulla oblongata fresh volume
cerebellum (measured without the pons) fresh volume
pons fresh volume
mesencephalon (incl. tectum + ventral tegmentum) fresh volume
diencephalon fresh volume
telencephalon fresh volume (sum of its components below)
neocortex fresh volume (gray without area striata + gray area striata + white matter)
neocortical gray matter excluding the gray of area striata
gray matter of area striata (primary visual cortex)
neocortical white matter fresh volume
hippocampus fresh volume
entorhinal region fresh volume
paleocortex fresh volume (regio praepiriformis + corpus amygdaloideum here)
regio praepiriformis (prepiriform cortex) fresh volume
corpus amygdaloideum (amygdala) fresh volume
septum fresh volume
corpus striatum (nucleus caudatus + putamen) fresh volume
globus pallidus (pallidum) fresh volume

fresh volumes printed in cc3 (= cm3); converted to mm3 by x1000. Percentages are of total brain volume excluding ventricles and nerves. Pongo body weight 54,000 g and fresh brain weight 333 g are given in the chapter text (Table 12-1), not in Table 12-2.
Note: Indices of brain-structure size (encephalization) are in Table 12-3, not snapshotted here (derived; recompute downstream).
